reviewSummary: The assembled graph merges 4 batches (252 nodes, 186 edges) but has significant issues: inconsistent ID formats from different batches, many missing source files, duplicate edges from import-map recovery, and orphaned nodes. The graph covers roughly 35-40% of actual source files.

issues.description: Five distinct node ID formats from different batches create ambiguity and prevent reliable cross-batch edge resolution.
details: Batch 1 (types/utils/dmgTypes/engine/data): 'file:src/lib/types.ts', 'function:__nofilepath__:src/lib/types.ts::applyUpgrade', 'file:src/lib/types.ts::StatKey'

Batch 2 (Boost/BuffData/TypedDmgBoost/perkAutoDebuffs): 'file::src/data/Boost.ts', 'function:__nofilepath__:node::src/data/Boost.ts::calcMinionAbsorptionPotency', 'file:node::src/data/Boost.ts::BoostContext'
Batch 8 (Svelte/data legacy): 'file:src/BaseDamageCalc.svelte', 'file:src/BaseDamageCalc.svelte#calcArmorMult', 'file:type:BuildState'

Batch 9 (weaponArts/constants/components): 'file::src/data/weaponArts.ts', 'file:interface::src/data/weaponArts.ts::WAIndividualHit', 'function::src/data/weaponConditionalBoosts.ts::getWeaponConditionalBoost'
Import map recovered edges use 'file:src/lib/store.ts' (with file: prefix) while batch-1 edges use bare 'src/lib/store.ts' as source
issues.impact: Edges between batches may silently fail to resolve. Downstream consumers parsing node IDs will get inconsistent results.

issues.description: All 10 import-map recovered edges duplicate existing edges from batch 1, inflating edge count from 176 to 186 with 10 phantom duplicates.
details: Duplicate pair: edge 'src/lib/store.ts->src/lib/types.ts::import' (line 2370) + recovered 'file:src/lib/store.ts -> file:src/lib/types.ts' (line 3668)
Duplicate pair: edge 'src/lib/utils.ts->src/lib/types.ts::import' (line 2379) + recovered 'file:src/lib/utils.ts -> file:src/lib/types.ts' (line 3676)
Duplicate pair: edge 'src/lib/dmgTypes.ts->src/lib/types.ts::import' (line 2331) + recovered 'file:src/lib/dmgTypes.ts -> file:src/lib/types.ts' (line 3684)
Duplicate pair: edge 'src/lib/damageTypeResolve.ts->src/lib/types.ts::import' (line 2323) + recovered 'file:src/lib/damageTypeResolve.ts -> file:src/lib/types.ts' (line 3692)
Duplicate pair: edge 'src/lib/engine/format.ts->src/lib/types.ts::import' (line 2363) + recovered 'file:src/lib/engine/format.ts -> file:src/lib/types.ts' (line 3700)
Duplicate pair: edge 'src/lib/engine/data/character.ts->src/lib/types.ts::import' (line 2339) + recovered 'file:src/lib/engine/data/character.ts -> file:src/lib/types.ts' (line 3708)
Duplicate pair: edge 'src/lib/engine/data/equipment.ts->src/lib/types.ts::import' (line 2347) + recovered 'file:src/lib/engine/data/equipment.ts -> file:src/lib/types.ts' (line 3716)
Duplicate pair: edge 'src/lib/engine/data/weapons.ts->src/lib/types.ts::import' (line 2355) + recovered 'file:src/lib/engine/data/weapons.ts -> file:src/lib/types.ts' (line 3724)
Duplicate pair: edge 'src/data/statboost.ts->src/lib/types.ts::import' (line 2315) + recovered 'file:src/data/statboost.ts -> file:src/lib/types.ts' (line 3740)
Recovered edge for Perkbasedmg.ts->types.ts (line 3732) has no corresponding batch-1 edge but IS correct per import map
issues.impact: Edge count inflated. Duplicate edges will confuse traversal algorithms and analytics.

issues.description: Batch 8 created duplicate type nodes with 'file:type:' IDs that overlap existing 'file:src/lib/types.ts::*' nodes from batch 1.
details: DUPLICATE: 'file:type:BuildState' (line 1659) duplicates 'file:src/lib/types.ts::BuildState' (line 169)
DUPLICATE: 'file:type:Enchantment' (line 1675) duplicates 'file:src/lib/types.ts::Enchantment' (line 124)
ORPHANED: 'file:type:WeaponStats' (line 1651) has no corresponding node from batch 1 — it may be a legitimate type not captured in batch 1, or a phantom type (should be verified against source)
ORPHANED: 'file:type:DamageResult' (line 1667) has no corresponding node from batch 1 — same concern as WeaponStats
issues.impact: Conceptual duplicates inflate node count and create ambiguous references. Edges may target either copy.

issues.description: Many significant source files have no node in the graph, including core engine modules, data modules, and Svelte components.

missing_core_engine: src/lib/engine.ts (barrel re-export module, imported by store.ts and App.svelte)
src/lib/engine/enchant.ts (applyEnchantmentsToSlot, applyPerkEffectiveness, applyInfusion — imported by build.ts)
src/lib/engine/data.ts (barrel for character/equipment/weapons — imported by build.ts)
missing_data_modules: src/data/DoTDamage.ts (imported by Perkbasedmg.ts per import map)
missing_svelte_components: src/lib/AppHeader.svelte (has node from batch 9 but NOT from App.svelte perspective)

issues.impact: Graph misses ~60% of source files. Critical engine pipeline files (weapon.ts, enchant.ts, shrine.ts, dmgTypeBonuses.ts) are absent, breaking the build calculation dependency chain visualization.

issues.description: Node metadata shape varies across batches: batch 1 wraps metadata in a 'metadata' object, batches 2 and 9 use flat top-level fields, batch 8 uses yet another set of field names.
details: Batch 1: { metadata: { language, fileCategory, sizeLines, description } }
Batch 2: { description, path, lineCount, language } (flat, no metadata wrapper)
Batch 8: { filePath, size, lines } (flat, uses 'size' instead of 'sizeLines' or 'lineCount')
Batch 9: { description, path, lineCount } (flat, no metadata wrapper)
issues.impact: Downstream consumers must handle 3+ metadata schemas. Automated analysis tools will fail on missing expected fields.

issues.description: The 'concept:ui-components' node (line 1704) has no edges connecting to or from it.
details: All other concept nodes (damage-calculation, build-management, game-data, cdr-cooldown, armor-system, perk-system, enchantment-system, moveset-system, mastery-system) have at least one incoming 'related' edge
UI components exist in the project (Svelte files, lib/ui/) but none reference this concept
issues.impact: Dead node in the graph. Either connect it to relevant UI files or remove it.

issues.description: Many verified import relationships from build.ts and App.svelte are absent from the graph.
details: build.ts -> src/data/cdr.ts: imports CDR_PERK_DATA (no edge)
build.ts -> src/data/raceEffects.ts: imports getActiveRaceEffect, calcOrkTenacityBonus (no edge)
build.ts -> src/lib/engine/enchant.ts: imports applyEnchantmentsToSlot, applyPerkEffectiveness, applyInfusion (no edge)
build.ts -> src/lib/engine/shrine.ts: imports applyShrineToStats, SHRINE_MULTIPLIERS (no edge)
build.ts -> src/lib/engine/weapon.ts: imports MONK_RANK_MULTIPLIER, calcWeapon (no edge)
build.ts -> src/lib/engine/dmgTypeBonuses.ts: imports buildDmgTypeBonuses (no edge)
build.ts -> src/lib/engine/data.ts: imports via barrel (no edge)
App.svelte -> src/lib/engine.ts: imports engine barrel (no edge)
App.svelte -> src/lib/EnchantSelect.svelte: imports component (no edge)
App.svelte -> src/lib/enchantTooltip.ts: imports getEnchantTooltipText (no edge)
App.svelte -> src/data/weaponArts.ts: imports WEAPON_ARTS, WeaponArt type (no edge)
App.svelte -> src/data/statboost.ts: imports OFFENSIVE_BOOSTS (no edge)
store.ts -> src/lib/engine.ts: imports calcBuild, races, enforceEnchantSlot via barrel (no edge)
issues.impact: Build calculation pipeline dependency chain is incomplete. import-map recovery only covered files to types.ts, not cross-module imports.

issues.description: All 10 import-map recovered edges lack an 'id' field, unlike all other edges in the graph.
details: Standard edges have: { id, source, target, type, label, direction }
Recovered edges have: { source, target, type, label, direction, weight, recoveredFromImportMap } — no 'id'
issues.impact: May cause issues for consumers expecting all edges to have stable IDs.

issues.description: Node types 'constant' (34), 'interface' (31), 'type' (11), and 'component' (7) are not in the formal schema but are semantically valid for a code knowledge graph.
details: These types were emitted by batch analyzers as sub-file-level entities
They are useful for granular dependency tracking but may confuse schema-strict consumers
Recommendation: formally extend the schema to include these types
issues.impact: Schema validation tools may flag these as errors.

issues.description: Edge ID formats vary: batch 1 uses 'source->target::type', batch 2 uses no IDs or different conventions, batch 8 uses no IDs.
details: Batch 1 edges: 'src/data/statboost.ts->src/lib/types.ts::import'
Batch 2 edges: no id field on most edges
Batch 8/9 edges: no id field on most edges
issues.impact: Edge deduplication requires ID generation rather than simple ID matching.

issues.description: All 252 nodes have complexity set to 'moderate', which is clearly not accurate for many nodes.
details: formatStat (14 lines) and calcBuild (552 lines) are both 'moderate'
Simple constants like BASE_HP (1 line) and complex pipelines like assembleActiveBuffs (50+ lines) share the same complexity
This was noted in the merge report as '252 → complexity None → defaulted to moderate'
issues.impact: Complexity metadata is non-informative. Needs per-node complexity assignment.

issues.description: Batch 8 nodes reference files like Allperks.ts, Allweapons.ts, ArmorData.ts etc. that exist in the graph but their file paths use absolute Windows paths (D:\...) while the rest of the graph uses relative paths.
details: file:src/data/Allperks.ts has filePath='D:\voxlblade-builder\src\data\Allperks.ts'
These absolute-path nodes also lack the 'metadata' wrapper from batch 1
They use 'size' and 'lines' instead of 'sizeLines'
issues.impact: Path normalization needed for consistent file referencing.

warnings.description: The graph has no edges for the engine barrel module (src/lib/engine.ts) which re-exports calcBuild, races, and many other symbols. Multiple files import through this barrel.
Edge direction is always 'forward' — the graph is a DAG. If bidirectional traversal is needed, all edges would need to be mirrored.

The 'child' edge type (App.svelte -> BaseDamageCalc.svelte etc.) duplicates the 'import' edge type for the same source->target pairs. Both exist: edge at line 3024 (import) and line 3227 (child) for App.svelte -> BaseDamageCalc.svelte.
Some batch 2 edges have extra fields ('details') not present in batch 1 edges, creating inconsistent edge shapes.

suggestions.action: Normalize all node IDs to a single format: 'file:<relative-path>' for files, 'file:<path>::<symbol>' for symbols, 'function:<path>::<name>' for functions. Strip __nofilepath__ and node:: prefixes.
suggestions.impact: Fixes the most critical issue — cross-batch ID incompatibility.

suggestions.action: Deduplicate nodes: remove file:type:BuildState, file:type:Enchantment, file:type:WeaponStats, file:type:DamageResult and merge their edges into the canonical file:src/lib/types.ts::* nodes.
suggestions.impact: Reduces node count by ~4 and prevents ambiguous references.

suggestions.action: Deduplicate import edges: remove the 9 import-map recovered edges that duplicate existing batch-1 import edges. Keep only the Perkbasedmg.ts->types.ts recovered edge.
suggestions.impact: Reduces edge count by 9, eliminates duplicate traversal paths.

suggestions.action: Add missing core engine files as nodes: engine.ts, engine/weapon.ts, engine/enchant.ts, engine/shrine.ts, engine/dmgTypeBonuses.ts, engine/data.ts, data/cdr.ts, data/raceEffects.ts.
suggestions.impact: Covers the critical build calculation pipeline that is currently invisible.

suggestions.action: Add missing import edges for build.ts -> engine sub-modules (enchant, shrine, weapon, dmgTypeBonuses, cdr, raceEffects) and App.svelte -> engine barrel, EnchantSelect, enchantTooltip.
suggestions.impact: Completes the dependency chain for the core build pipeline.

suggestions.action: Normalize node metadata: standardize on either flat fields or a metadata wrapper. Use consistent field names (lineCount vs sizeLines, filePath vs path).
Either connect concept:ui-components to relevant files (App.svelte, lib/ui/*.svelte, component nodes) or remove the orphaned concept node.
Remove duplicate 'child' edges where an equivalent 'import' edge already exists (App.svelte -> child components).
Assign meaningful complexity values (trivial/simple/moderate/complex/critical) based on lines of code, cyclomatic complexity, or fan-in/fan-out instead of defaulting everything to 'moderate'.
suggestions.impact: Makes complexity metadata useful for prioritization.

suggestions.action: Add 'id' fields to import-map recovered edges for consistency with all other edges.

Add missing data modules as nodes for completeness: DoTDamage.ts, HealBoost.ts, SummonData.ts, Runebasedmg.ts, debuffCombatEffects.ts, defensivePerks.ts, procCoefficients.ts, selfDamagePerks.ts, stanceOverlays.ts, Weaponartcheck.ts.
suggestions.impact: Improves coverage from ~40% to ~70% of source files.
